title: Decadent Citrusy Middle Eastern Goose Fatteh
description: A spring-inspired brunch dish featuring the rich flavors of goose fat, coconut cream, and citrusy notes.
instructions: Heat the goose fat in a skillet over medium heat to melt and bubble.
Add the crumbled pita bread and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly toasted and crispy.
Stir in dried rose petals and cook for 1 minute, allowing their fragrance to infuse the fat.
Remove from heat and stir in coconut cream until combined and smooth.
Add freshly squeezed orange juice, grated ginger, honey, and salt, stirring to combine.
Transfer to a serving dish and garnish with fresh parsley, lemon wedges, and juicy pomegranate seeds.
Serve warm, allowing each guest to assemble their own fatteh with a dollop of yogurt, if desired.
Gather around the table and enjoy the conversation, as the warm, spicy aromas transport you to a Middle Eastern spring market.
For a fun twist, set up a DIY fatteh bar with an assortment of toppings, such as pickled turnips, chopped fresh herbs, and crumbled sumac.
Gather your family and friends, and savor the delightful flavors and textures of this traditional Middle Eastern fatteh, elevated with the richness of goose fat and the sweetness of oranges.
Make it a special occasion and serve with a side of warm, fluffy pita bread and a dollop of creamy tzatziki sauce.
To make it ahead, assemble the fatteh up to a day in advance, then refrigerate and reheat when needed.
Reheat the fatteh gently over low heat, or until warmed through and the pita bread is crispy and golden.
Gather your loved ones and dig in!
